10: Following local optimization, MASS optimizes the
11: workflow topology by selecting and arranging different agent interactions from a
12: customizable design space. To make this search efficient, MASS employs an
13: influence-weighted method. This method calculates the "incremental influence" of
14: each topology by measuring its performance gain relative to a baseline agent and
15: uses these scores to guide the search toward more promising combinations. For
16: instance, when optimizing for the MBPP coding task, the topology search discovers
17: that a specific hybrid workflow is most effective. The best-found topology is not a
18: simple structure but a combination of an iterative refinement process with external
19: tool use. Specifically, it consists of one predictor agent that engages in several rounds
20: of reflection, with its code being verified by one executor agent that runs the code
21: against test cases. This discovered workflow shows that for coding, a structure that
22: combines iterative self-correction with external verification is superior to simpler MAS
